P A G F. 0 N f

MARCH WILL BE ON


TUESDAY

pages.raw_text:
protest Tuesday night is believed
certain by the leaders, who have
felt the pulse of the workers of
Spokane, who, without a dissenting
voice, say they are tired of the
lodging and postponement of the
council.


Either we are going to get what
we want, Tuesday night, or there
are going to be some new faces in
the city council. and if the mayor
wants to discover the temper of the
workers of Spokane all he needs do
is to attempt to postpone action on
the wage and matrOn questions UNI
til Wednesday," said MI Coates at
loon today.


The parade will form at 1880 and
march on the city hall, and every
Spokane union is ordered into the
parade.


1t is the first time in the history
of the city that a body of 5000 citi-
zens, demanding justice, have
marched on the city hall, and union
leaders apparently have no especial
fear of Chief sullivan doing any
clubbing, or of there being any Jail
coils filled with enthusiastic union
men.


1--WE can't get anything out of the
council without soaring them; they
have repeatedly gone back on their
solemn pledges and rescinded their
ordinances proposing a square deal.
The only way out now is to fight,
and We are going to fight


That is the way another member
of the protest parade committee
feels about it.


Next Tuesday night will be the
liyeliest evening Spokane has eX-
perienced in months, and without
definite action from the council on
the question of an increase for the
city laborers and on the police ma
iron problem the last attempt of
labor to politely request things
from the council will have been
made. 1t will be recall a special
election, and the putting in office
of fair men-that is the last word
Of labor.
